references: EDB English Language Education Curriculum Guide (Primary 1-6) (2025)
EDB primary English language skills development resources for Key Stage 1
EDB English vocabulary wordlist preamble and theme-based vocabulary guidance
source.note: No fixed official Hong Kong P1 vocabulary list is prescribed; this list is theme-based for lower-primary English enrichment import. Term-only entries are completed during import.

words.definition: A word you say when you meet someone.
A word you say when you leave.
A polite word when you ask for something.
To show that you are grateful.
A word you say when you make a mistake.
Used to agree or say something is true.
Used to disagree or refuse.
The person who is speaking.
The person you are talking to.
Belonging to me.
What someone or something is called.
A male child.
A female child.
A person you like and play with.
A person who helps children learn at school.
A male parent.
A female parent.
A boy in the same family.
A girl in the same family.
A very young child.
The number 1.
The number 2.
The number 3.
The number 4.
The number 5.
The number 6.
The number 7.
words.example: There are seven days in a week.

words.definition: The number 8.
The number 9.
The number 10.
The colour of apples or fire trucks.
The colour of the sky on a clear day.
The colour of grass and leaves.
The colour of bananas or the sun.
The darkest colour.
The colour of milk or snow.
Something you read with pages inside.
A tool used for writing with ink.
A tool used for writing and drawing.
words.example: Draw a cat with a pencil.

words.definition: Something you use to carry books.
A table you use at school.
words.example: The book is on the desk.

words.definition: Something you sit on.
You open it to go in or out of a room.
An opening in a wall with glass.
A straight tool used to measure lines.
words.example: I draw a line with a ruler.

words.definition: Something that removes pencil marks.
words.example: Use a rubber to erase it.

words.definition: The top part of your body with your face.
The body part you use to see.
The body part you use to hear.
The body part you use to smell.
The body part you use to eat and speak.
The body part at the end of your arm.
The part of the body between the shoulder and the hand.
The part of the body you use to walk.
The body part at the end of your leg.
A small furry pet that says meow.
A common pet that barks.
An animal with wings and feathers.
An animal that lives in water.
words.example: The fish is in the water.

words.definition: A farm animal that says oink.
A farm animal that gives milk.
A bird that swims and says quack.
words.example: The duck is in the pond.

words.definition: A female chicken.
A round fruit that is often red or green.
A long yellow fruit.
A round orange-coloured fruit.
Food that comes from a hen.
words.example: I eat an egg for breakfast.

words.definition: A white drink from cows.
Food made from flour, often used for sandwiches.
Small white grains eaten as food.
A clear drink that people need every day.
A place where children learn.
words.example: I go to school every day.

words.definition: The place where you live.
A space inside a building.
To move quickly on your feet.
To push yourself up off the ground.
To move at a normal speed on your feet.
To rest on a chair or the ground.
To be on your feet, not sitting.
To look at words and understand them.
To make words with a pen or pencil.
To make a picture with a pencil or pen.
To make music with your voice.
To move your body to music.
To have fun or do a game.
words.example: The children play in the playground.

words.definition: To put food in your mouth.
To swallow liquid.
To rest with your eyes closed at night.
To use your eyes to see something.
To use your ears to hear.
To say words.
Large in size.
Little in size.
Not short.
Not long.
Feeling good and smiling.
Feeling unhappy.
Nice or well done.
Not old; recently made.
Not new; having lived for a long time.
Having a high temperature.
Having a low temperature.
To enjoy something.
To be able to do something.
To move from one place to another.
To move toward someone.
